latest.title: Replacement: Capital Reduction Update
latest.content:
4 Mar 2026 13:20
RNS Number : 3511V
IG Design Group PLC
04 March 2026
The following amendment has been made to the Capital Reduction Update announcement released on 4 March 2026 at 12:15 under RNS No 3439V ("the Announcement").
The Announcement should have been dated 4 March 2026. This amendment has been made below.
All other details remain unchanged.
4 March 2026
IG Design Group plc
(the "Company", the "Group" or "Design Group")
Capital Reduction Update
The board of directors of IG Design Group plc is pleased to announce that the reduction of capital (the "
Capital Reduction
") required to effect the cancellation of the Capital Reduction Shares issued by the Company prior to the Court Hearing together with share premium and the capital redemption reserve was approved on 3 March 2026 by the High Court of Justice of England and Wales (the "
Court
").
The Court order confirming the Capital Reduction and a statement of capital approved by the Court in connection therewith will shortly be sent to the Registrar of Companies. The Capital Reduction will become effective upon the registration of the Court order and associated statement of capital by the Registrar of Companies. Completion of the Capital Reduction will not affect the rights attaching to the Ordinary Shares and will not result in any change to the number of Ordinary Shares in issue (or their nominal value).
The Capital Reduction will allow the Company to potentially make future shareholder distributions.
Full details of the Capital Reduction are set out in the notice of extraordinary general meeting which was sent to shareholders of the Company on 15 January 2026 (the "
EGM Circular
"). The EGM Circular is also available for inspection on the Company website,
Reports and Presentations - Design Group
. Unless otherwise stated, defined terms in this announcement are the same as those set out in the EGM Circular. As set out in the EGM Circular, provisional dates were previously obtained by the Company for the required Court Hearings, which were subsequently changed at the discretion of the Company.
For further information, please contact:
IG Design Group plc
Tel: +44 (0)1525 887310
Stewart Gilliland, Interim Executive Chair
Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Alma Strategic Communications
Tel: +44 (0)20 3405 0205
Rebecca Sanders-Hewett
designgroup@almastrategic.com
Sam Modlin
Will Merison

latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4928S
IG Design Group PLC
11 February 2026
11 February 2026
IG Design Group plc
(the "Company", the "Group" or "Design Group")
Trading update
Revenue, profit and cash ahead of expectations
IG Design Group plc, a leading designer, innovator and manufacturer across various celebration and creative categories, issues a trading update to 31 December 2025, and an update on its outlook. The trading update refers to the continuing business of the Group only, excluding adjusting items and losses related to the disposal of DG Americas announced in May 2025.
The Board is pleased to report that trading for the nine months ended 31 December 2025, including performance over the Christmas trading period, has been in line with expectations.
For the full financial year ending 31 March 2026 ("FY2026"), the Board now anticipates being at the upper end of the previously stated guidance* and above current market consensus**. FY2026 revenues are anticipated to be c.$280-285 million, with final quarter sales expected to be in line with prior year. Adjusted operating margin is also expected to be at the higher end of expectation at c.4%. Together with interest income above current market expectations, this is expected to result in adjusted profit exceeding current market expectations.
Cash performance during the year has been particularly strong, with full-year net cash expected to be c.$55-60 million, which is c.$15-20 million ahead of market expectations*. This outperformance reflects disciplined cash and working capital management across the continuing Group following the disposal of DG Americas. In addition, the sale of a surplus UK warehouse has been provisionally agreed and is expected to close by year end, further strengthening the cash position.
Looking beyond FY2026, the Board's outlook remains consistent with prior guidance. The underlying business is expected to deliver annual revenue growth of 0-5%, adjusted operating margins of 4-5% and sustainable cash generation of $6-8 million per annum. Alongside this, the Group continues to focus on its long-term growth pillars which are expected to drive incremental value creation beyond the underlying trading performance.
On 4 February 2026, the Company announced that all resolutions put to shareholders relating to the capital reduction, to create additional distributable reserves that would allow the Company to make future shareholder distributions, had been approved. The Group expects to publish its FY2026 results in June 2026, which will include an update on its future capital allocation policy.
The CEO recruitment process remains underway. Stewart Gilliland remains committed to the role of Interim Executive Chair until an appointment has been finalised.
The Group intends to adopt GBP as its presentation currency for consolidated financial statements, replacing USD, effective from the FY2026 full-year results.
*
Previously stated guidance: revenue $270-280 million, adjusted operating margin 3-4% and cash $40-45 million.
**
Current market consensus: revenue $275 million, adjusted operating profit $9.7 million, adjusted profit $7.6 million and cash $42 million.
For further information, please contact:
​
IG Design Group plc
Tel: +44 (0)1525 887310
Stewart Gilliland, Interim Executive Chair
Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer
​
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7523 8000

company_30s.what_they_do: IG Design Group makes and sells celebratory and party‑related products such as decorations, balloons, and themed accessories for events and celebrations.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The party‑goods market is a steady, seasonal niche that grows with consumer spending on events, weddings, and corporate celebrations. The company’s designs are used by retailers and event planners, giving it a foothold in a market that rarely sees dramatic price swings.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is undergoing a major capital restructuring and has recently announced a capital reduction, a new debt facility, and asset disposals. These moves signal that it is burning cash and trying to shore up balance‑sheet health while keeping operations running.

rns_narrative: The company has been focused on restructuring its balance sheet – a series of capital‑reduction proposals and an extraordinary general meeting show that shareholders are trying to reduce debt and bring the business back to a sustainable footing.
Operational updates, such as the latest trading update and interim results, indicate that the business is still generating revenue, but the figures point to thin margins and a need for fresh capital.
Governance changes – board appointments, new debt, and the sale of a distribution facility – suggest the firm is trimming excess and concentrating on core product lines.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet hope – the chatter is minimal but the few bullish voices point to a cheap entry.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are praising the low price, calling it a “penny opportunity” and noting that the company’s core product line has steady demand.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Critics warn that the lack of a clear notice for updates gives market makers an edge, and that the repeated capital‑reduction cycle could signal deeper liquidity issues.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The conversation has moved from neutral to mildly bullish as recent posts highlight the company’s willingness to cut costs and raise fresh capital.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent spikes, which means a good chance of surprise if a catalyst arrives.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the capital‑reduction plan and the closing of the new debt facility – if the company can secure the funds, it will free up cash for operations.
A positive quarterly earnings release that shows improved cash flow or a new product line launch for the upcoming holiday season.
A volume spike that breaks through the recent support level, indicating that traders are starting to see the stock as a buy.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained 3‑day volume increase above the 20‑day average, a bounce off the low‑crash support, and any mention of a new partnership or contract that could lift demand.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early stages of a mean‑reversion cycle – the APEX score suggests tight compression, but the price is still well below its pre‑crash level. A bounce that reaches halfway to the former high would trigger the core thesis.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The 86% plunge puts the stock deep in the RED zone, historically the most rewarding for a contrarian. If the price climbs to 50% of its pre‑crash level, the upside is roughly 100% – a risk‑reversal that has been seen in similar deep‑crash scenarios. The recent capital restructuring gives the company a chance to recover cash flow and stabilize.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the company fails to close the debt facility or that revenue does not improve, leaving the firm with a thin balance sheet. In that scenario the stock could stay near the bottom or fall further if a liquidation is triggered.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current low price – a small percentage of any portfolio. Potential gain is double or triple that if the stock rebounds to mid‑pre‑crash levels, creating a 2:1 or 3:1 reward‑to‑risk ratio.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the recent low with a 3‑day volume confirmation. Size the position to 5–10% of your discretionary cash and place a stop just below the support level to cap losses.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the debt facility closes or until earnings show improved cash flow. Watch for a 50% volume increase and a price touch of the low‑crash support as entry signals.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or is delisted, exit immediately. No other terminal risks are identified at this time.

ai_insights.bottom_line: IGR’s steep 86% plunge, dead market chatter, and upcoming capital restructuring create a high‑asymmetry contrarian play that could deliver a 200%+ upside if the stock rebounds to mid‑pre‑crash levels.
